metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific file(s) or locations (e.g., docstrings in each test function, or a named inventory doc) where the 'supplemental/structural' label must appear for AC4, so an executor can act and a reviewer can verify without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The 'coverage inventory' target for AC4's labeling change is not identified as a specific file or section; the Scope mentions 'coverage-inventory notes / docstrings' without naming the file where the label must appear, leaving an executor unable to act without guessing the target artifact.
findings.location: Scope bullet: 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard...' and AC4: 'labeled supplemental/structural in the coverage inventory'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete target to AC4, e.g.: 'Add a `# STRUCTURAL TRIPWIRE — supplemental, not behavioral coverage` comment to each test function's docstring' or 'Update `docs/coverage-inventory.md` row for each test to read `supplemental/structural`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file(s) that constitute the 'coverage inventory' for AC4's labeling change (e.g. docs/coverage.md, a specific section, or confirm the existing test docstrings already satisfy it).
findings.finding: AC4 requires labeling the three tripwire tests as 'supplemental/structural in the coverage inventory', but no specific file is named as the 'coverage inventory'. The test docstrings already bear 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard' labels; docs/coverage.md contains no mention of these tests. The plan never identifies which concrete artifact (file path + section) is the coverage inventory that needs updating.
findings.location: AC4 / Scope: 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add the three test names with 'supplemental/structural' labels to docs/coverage.md under a new subsection, or (b) revise AC4 to state 'the existing SUPPLEMENTAL labels in the test docstrings satisfy this criterion (no additional inventory file needed)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file(s) and section(s) where the 'supplemental/structural' label must appear for AC4 (e.g. specify whether `docs/coverage.md`, the test module docstrings, or both are the targets, and confirm whether `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf` also requires an explicit 'supplemental' label).
findings.finding: AC4's mechanism is underspecified: the 'coverage inventory' where the three tripwire tests must be labeled 'supplemental/structural' is never named. Two of the three test files already contain 'SUPPLEMENTAL' labels in their docstrings (test_leaves_registry_coverage.py and test_llm_timeouts.py), so an implementer may conclude AC4 is already satisfied and make no further change — while docs/coverage.md (the only candidate external inventory) has no mention of these tests at all. The third test (test_matrix_covers_every_leaf) uses 'structural' terminology in its module docstring but carries no explicit 'supplemental' label. Without naming the target file/section, the implementer cannot determine what concrete edit closes this AC.
findings.location: AC4 / Scope section ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan')
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC4 bullet, add: 'The labeling targets are the test module/function docstrings (already done for test_leaves_registry_coverage.py and test_llm_timeouts.py) and a note in docs/coverage.md if that file is used as a coverage inventory; confirm which file(s) need editing. Also confirm whether test_matrix_covers_every_leaf requires an explicit supplemental label in its docstring (currently only uses structural terminology).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC4's labeling target: name the specific file/section (e.g., docs/coverage.md, a section of docs/mutation-testing.md, or the test docstrings) that still needs the 'supplemental/structural' label, and verify that target does not already carry it — two of the three test docstrings already read 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard.'
findings.finding: The plan's AC4 states the three named tripwire tests need to be 'labeled supplemental/structural in the coverage inventory,' implying this labeling is absent. However, two of the three tests already carry 'SUPPLEMENTAL' in their own docstrings: test_every_leaf_has_nonstub_body_structural_guard (module docstring: 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard for applier._LEAVES') and test_no_total_runtime_timer_mechanism (docstring: 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard — NOT counted as behavioral liveness coverage'). If the labeling target is the test docstrings, the work for these two is already done and the plan overstates remaining effort; if the target is a separate coverage inventory document (e.g., docs/coverage.md), that document is never named and has no mention of any of the three tests.
findings.location: AC4 / Scope ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan')
findings.suggested_fix: Since docs/coverage.md has no entry for any of the three tripwire tests, the concrete gap is there. Revise AC4 to say: 'Add a note to docs/coverage.md (or a named section of docs/mutation-testing.md) listing the three tripwire tests as supplemental/structural and clarifying they do not count toward behavioral coverage.' Remove any implication that docstring labeling remains outstanding for the two tests that already carry it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file (e.g., `docs/coverage-inventory.md` or the relevant section of an existing doc) where the three tripwire tests will be labeled 'supplemental/structural', and add it to the Scope list.
findings.finding: The coverage-inventory artifact where the three tripwire tests are to be labeled 'supplemental/structural' is never named. The Scope lists 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan' but the AC simultaneously states 'the tests are not modified,' which means the labels must land in a separate document — yet no such document is identified or listed in Scope.
findings.location: Scope section; AC bullet 4 ('This is a labeling/inventory change only; the tests are not modified.')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the Scope section such as: `docs/coverage-inventory.md` (or wherever the existing inventory lives) — and update AC bullet 4 to read '...are labeled supplemental/structural in `docs/coverage-inventory.md` (no behavioral-coverage credit)'. If no standalone inventory file exists yet, note that one should be created as part of this task.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the conflict between Scope ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings') and AC4 ('tests are not modified'): either (a) name the specific non-test file (e.g. a coverage-inventory markdown or a conftest comment) where the supplemental labels will be added, or (b) explicitly clarify that docstring edits inside test files are permitted and adjust AC4's wording accordingly.
findings.finding: Scope says to update 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan', which implies editing content inside or alongside the named test files. But AC4 explicitly states 'the tests are not modified.' If docstrings live inside the test files, this is a direct contradiction between Scope and AC4; if they live elsewhere, the Scope lacks a specific file path, leaving the implementer without a clear target.
findings.location: Scope section ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings...') vs. AC item 4 ('the tests are not modified')
findings.suggested_fix: If the labels are to be added as comments/docstrings within the test files, change AC4's parenthetical to 'the test logic is not modified (docstring/comment labeling is permitted)'. If a separate inventory document is intended, add its path to Scope (e.g. docs/coverage-inventory.md) and add a sub-bullet to AC4 confirming that file is updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and correct the second util-linux flock instance at README.md line 198 (Install section) in addition to lines 146-151.
findings.finding: README.md contains a second instance of the util-linux/BusyBox external-flock framing at line 198 ('a util-linux flock is used for write serialization when present, with a mkdir fallback otherwise') in the Install section — outside the plan's stated target range of lines 146-151. This consumer of the same correction is not enumerated, so the README fix may be incomplete.
findings.location: README.md line 198 / AC: 'README.md describes the current Python fcntl + mkdir dual-window lock and drops the util-linux/BusyBox external-flock framing'
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'Also update README.md ~line 198 to remove the util-linux framing in the Install section summary.' to the Scope or AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each of ACs 1-4 (e.g., a `grep -n` or `grep -c` anchored to the expected/absent string) so that CI or an agent can produce binary evidence of each content change.
findings.finding: ACs 1-4 name no concrete proving command (e.g., grep pattern) that produces evidence of each specific text change; only `make lint` is listed, which checks format but does NOT verify the substantive claims (e.g., that concurrency.md now references the test name, or that README no longer mentions util-linux/BusyBox).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section, ACs 1-4; 'make lint passes (docs/format checks)'
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' subsection with commands such as:
```
# AC1
grep -n 'test_sync_preserves_detached_head_local_ahead_commit' docs/concurrency.md
# AC2 (absence)
! grep -iE 'util-linux|busybox|external.*flock|flock.*binary' README.md
# AC3
grep -n 'selected scope' docs/mutation-testing.md
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the title (or add a subtitle) to explicitly mention the coverage-inventory / tripwire-test labeling change, e.g. 'Correct stale docs: concurrency.md detached-HEAD, README flock, mutation scope, and tripwire-test inventory labels'.
findings.finding: The title names exactly three doc artifacts ('concurrency.md detached-HEAD, README flock, mutation scope') but the Scope section and AC add a fourth distinct artifact class — coverage-inventory notes and docstrings for three named tripwire tests — which goes slightly beyond what the title promises.
findings.location: Title vs. Scope section ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan') and AC bullet 4.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Correct stale docs: concurrency.md detached-HEAD, README flock, mutation scope, tripwire-test labels' — or add a one-sentence summary in the Why section that explicitly calls out the four correction targets.

findings.finding: ACs 1–4 have no proving commands beyond 'make lint', which only checks formatting — an agent could produce syntactically valid docs that still omit the required content changes and `make lint` would still pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs); Step 'make lint passes (docs/format checks)'
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to Acceptance Criteria:
- `grep -F 'test_sync_preserves_detached_head_local_ahead_commit' docs/concurrency.md` exits 0.
- `grep -F 'fcntl' README.md` exits 0 AND `grep -F 'flock' README.md | grep -v fcntl` exits 1.
- `grep -F 'selected scope' docs/mutation-testing.md` exits 0.
- `grep -iF 'supplemental' <path-to-coverage-inventory>` exits 0.

findings.finding: AC4 and the Scope's coverage-inventory note name three structural tripwire test artifacts to label supplemental ('leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan'), but only the leaf AST guard (test_leaves_registry_coverage.py) is locatable in the repo. The 'registry-completeness assertion' and 'source-string timer scan' produce zero hits across the entire test tree, so an implementer following AC4 cannot find two of the three artifacts the plan tasks them to update.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan'; AC4

findings.finding: The plan's Scope section names 'registry-completeness assertion' as a structural tripwire test whose coverage-inventory label must be updated (AC4), but no such test — by that name or any close variant — can be found in tests/ or docs/. Only two of the three named tripwires are locatable: 'leaf AST guard' (test_leaves_registry_coverage.py) and 'source-string timer scan' (test_no_total_runtime_timer_mechanism in test_llm_timeouts.py).
findings.location: Scope section: 'Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan'; AC4

findings.finding: The plan asserts three structural tripwire tests exist ('leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan') and requires their coverage-inventory labels be updated (AC4/Scope). Only one such test is found in the repo (test_leaves_registry_coverage.py — the leaf AST guard). Searches for 'registry-completeness assertion' and 'source-string timer scan' tests return no hits anywhere in tests/ or docs/. If these two tests do not exist, AC4 cannot be satisfied and the Scope section misnames the work.
findings.location: Scope section ('Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness assertion, and source-string timer scan') and AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Search for all test files containing 'supplemental' or 'structural' marker text, enumerate the exact three files/functions the plan intends, and replace the informal names in the Scope section and AC4 with their actual test function names and file paths.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
